programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health and home care. It coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid-covered care to avoid nursing homes.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified for nursing home care, able to live safely in community, meeting MaineCare/Medicare eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of approved services at no additional cost beyond premiums.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waivers
programs.what_it_does: These MaineCare-funded waivers provide in-home supportive services like personal care and homemaker services to eligible seniors and disabled adults. Services aim to promote independence and delay institutionalization, accessed via Maximus MED assessment.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and disabled adults eligible for nursing home level of care but preferring community living, meeting financial criteria.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; covers services like adult day, personal care based on assessed needs, potentially with co-pay.

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly EBT benefits to buy food for low-income households including seniors. In Maine, it's administered by DHHS with deductions for seniors' expenses.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ or disabled households meeting income tests (net income ≤100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household; max up to $291/single (2026 rates).

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help eligible low-income households, including seniors, meet heating costs. Maine's program includes one-time payments and crisis assistance.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (≤150% FPL, higher for elderly/disabled) with high energy burdens.
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,500 one-time grant depending on income, household size, fuel type.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation and sealing for low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Maine administers federally funded services prioritizing seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income (≤200% FPL) owner/renter households, especially elderly, disabled, children.
programs.benefit_value: Free services saving 10-30% on energy bills; ~$5,000 average per home investment.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP) - Maine Senior Health Insurance Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free counseling on Medicare, Medigap, and related options. Maine's program helps seniors select plans and resolve issues.
programs.who_its_for: All Maine Medicare beneficiaries 65+, disabled, or caregivers seeking insurance guidance.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; potential savings of thousands on premiums/coverage gaps.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious meals delivered to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Funded via MaineCare, AoA, and state sources through aging agencies.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, meeting minimal income guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at low/no cost (suggested donation $3/meal); ~$1,500/yr value.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: SCSEP provides part-time paid community service training leading to employment for low-income seniors. AmeriCorps Seniors operates Foster Grandparents in Maine regions.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income (≤125% FPL) adults 55+ unable to find work.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$10-15/hr, 20 hrs/wk) plus training; ~$10,000/yr.

programs.name: Maine Legal Services for the Elderly
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance to low-income seniors on issues like benefits, housing, abuse. Pine Tree Legal Aid partners for broader support.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ in Maine facing civil legal problems.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal representation; protects benefits/housing worth thousands.
